message:
fix(core): prevent spurious FK cascade on UPDATE when PK unchanged (#24)

When PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON, an UPDATE on a parent table was
incorrectly triggering ON DELETE CASCADE (and other FK actions) on child
tables even when the FK-referenced column (primary key) was not
modified. This silently destroyed child rows.

Root cause: check_fk_on_delete and check_fk_on_update used ALL parent
columns as the key when fk.parent_columns was empty (implicit rowid/IPK
reference). This meant any column change was treated as a PK change.

Fix: When fk.parent_columns is empty, resolve only the IPK column
(is_ipk=true) instead of all columns. The old-vs-new value comparison
then correctly skips cascading when the IPK is unchanged.

Also fixes: col_map 2-tuple to 3-tuple migration (build_join_col_map,
col_map_for_source, collect_update_trigger_rows, window function
col_map), adds missing `use std::borrow::Cow` import, and fixes
execute_explain argument type.

Adds 9 regression tests:
- UPDATE non-PK column preserves children (TEXT PK + IPK)
- UPDATE PK column correctly errors (ON UPDATE NO ACTION)
- UPDATE PK column correctly cascades (ON UPDATE CASCADE)
- Composite FK: UPDATE non-FK column preserves children
- Self-referencing FK: UPDATE non-FK column preserves tree
- ON DELETE SET NULL not triggered by non-PK UPDATE
- NULL FK values handled correctly during UPDATE
- Multiple child tables preserved on non-PK UPDATE

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
